subject: Biology
title: Plant Defenses
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Plant Defenses.

hook.text: Plants can't run from predators, so they fight back with thorns, toxins, and chemical warfare.

buildup.text: Roses have thorns. Nettles inject formic acid. Poison ivy triggers allergic skin reactions.

discovery.text: When caterpillars chew a leaf, the plant releases chemicals that attract predatory wasps.

twist.text: Some plants warn their neighbors. Damaged trees release airborne signals that trigger defenses nearby.

climax.text: Caffeine, nicotine, and capsaicin all evolved as plant pesticides—we just enjoy consuming them.

punchline.text: Plants fight wars. You just can't see them.

quiz.question: Why did plants evolve caffeine and nicotine?
